data.title: Rivian gains after reporting a gross profit for Q4, updating on mass market R2 launch
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) traded slightly higher on Thursday after reporting Q4 results and updating on its 2024 outlook. The company said it achieved record revenues in the quarter, driven by the sale of regulatory credits and software and services revenue growth, as well as increasing R1 average selling prices with the increased availability of its Tri-Motor offering.

Notably, Rivian (NASDAQ:RIVN) also narrowed its quarterly adjusted EBITDA loss compared to a year ago and reported a gross profit.

The electric vehicle maker had already announced that it produced 12,727 vehicles at its manufacturing facility in Normal, Illinois in Q4 and delivered 14,183 vehicles during the same period. Both those marks were ahead of the consensus estimate of analysts. On a full-year 2024 basis, the company produced 49,476 vehicles and delivered 51,579, which was roughly in line with management’s full-year 2024 guidance of 47,000 to 49,000 vehicles produced and 50,500 to 52,000 vehicles delivered.

The electric vehicle maker highlighted once again that the shortage of a shared component on the R1 and RCV platforms is no longer a constraint on its production.

CEO update: "This quarter we achieved positive gross profit and removed $31,000 in automotive cost of goods sold per vehicle delivered in Q4 2024 relative to Q4 2023. Our focus on cost efficiency across the business is critical for the launch of our mass market product, R2... External factors could impact Rivian's 2025 expectations, including changes to government policies and regulations and a challenging demand environment "

Looking ahead, Rivian (RIVN) set 2025 deliveries guidance at 46,000 to 51,000 vehicles. Adjusted EBITDA for the full year is expected to be -$1.7 billion to -$1.9 billion.

Shares of Rivian Automotive (RIVN) were up 2.6% in early investor reaction to the Q4 earnings report.

data.title: Coca-Cola Consolidated Non-GAAP EPS of $18.11, revenue of $1.74B
data.description:
• Coca-Cola Consolidated press release (NASDAQ:COKE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $18.11.
  • Revenue of $1.74B (+6.7% Y/Y).
  • Cash flows from operations for fiscal year 2024 were $876.4 million, compared to $810.7 million for fiscal year 2023.
  • Gross profit in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $698 million, an increase of 9% versus the fourth quarter of 2023. Gross margin in the fourth quarter of 2024 improved by 70 basis points(a) to 40%.
  • Income from operations for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $219 million, an increase of $40 million, or 23%, versus the fourth quarter of 2023. For fiscal year 2024, income from operations increased $86 million to $920 million.

data.title: Copart GAAP EPS of $0.40 beats by $0.03, revenue of $1.16B beats by $30M
data.description:
• Copart press release (NASDAQ:CPRT): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.40 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $1.16B (+13.7% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • For the six months ended January 31, 2025, revenue, gross profit, and net income attributable to Copart, Inc. were $2.31 billion, $1.0 billion, and $749.5 million, respectively. These represent an increase in revenue of $269.6 million, or 13.2%; an increase in gross profit of $109.5 million, or 11.8%; and an increase in net income attributable to Copart, Inc. of $91.3 million, or 13.9%, respectively, from the same period last year. Fully diluted earnings per share for the six months ended January 31, 2025 was $0.77 compared to $0.68 last year, an increase of 13.2%.

data.title: Grid Dynamics Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.02, revenue of $100.28M beats by $4.28M
data.description:
• Grid Dynamics press release (NASDAQ:GDYN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $100.28M (+28.4% Y/Y) beats by $4.28M.

  • Financial Outlook

    First Quarter

    • The Company expects revenues in the first quarter of 2025 to be in the range of $98.0 to $100.0 million, representing growth of 22.8% to 25.3% on a year-over-year basis. At the midpoint of $99.0 million, the Company expects its first quarter revenue to grow by 24.0% on an year-over-year basis.
    • Non-GAAP EBITDA in the first quarter of 2025 is expected to be between $12.9 and $13.9 million.
    • For the first quarter of 2025, we expect our basic share count to be in the 84.0-85.0 million range and diluted share count to be in the 89.0-90.0 million range.

    Full Year

    • The Company expects full year 2025 revenues to be in the range of $415.0 to $435.0 million, representing growth of 18.4% to 24.1% on a year-over-year basis. At the midpoint of $425.0 million, the Company expects the year-over-year growth rate for 2025 revenues to be 21.2%.

data.title: Airbus targets 820 aircraft deliveries and €7B EBIT adjusted for 2025 amid supply chain challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Airbus SE (OTCPK:EADSF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Guillaume Faury highlighted a strong order intake across all businesses, with 878 gross orders in 2024, including 211 in Q4. The A321XLR entered into service, unlocking new opportunities for customers. He emphasized that Airbus delivered 766 commercial aircraft in 2024, including 269 in Q4, reflecting a 4% year-on-year increase.
  • Faury noted a focus on transforming the Defence and Space division, including exploratory discussions with Thales and Leonardo to evaluate strategic options for consolidating the European space sector.
  • CFO Thomas Toepfer reported full-year revenues of €69.2 billion, a 6% year-on-year increase, driven by higher deliveries and growth in the Air Power business. EBIT adjusted for 2024 amounted to €5.4 billion, impacted by €1.3 billion in charges related to space programs.
  • Toepfer announced a dividend proposal of €2 per share and a special dividend of €1 per share, reflecting confidence in future financial performance.

Outlook

  • Airbus targets around 820 commercial aircraft deliveries, EBIT adjusted of approximately €7 billion, and free cash flow before customer financing of €4.5 billion for 2025.
  • The company plans to ramp up production rates for the A320 family to 75 per month by 2027, while the A350 freighter variant’s entry-into-service is now expected in the second half of 2027.
  • Management acknowledged ongoing supply chain challenges, particularly with Spirit AeroSystems and engine suppliers, which are expected to impact Q1 2025 deliveries but are anticipated to normalize in the second half of the year.

Financial Results

  • Full-year 2024 revenues reached €69.2 billion, supported by increased aircraft and helicopter deliveries and higher volumes in the Air Power business.
  • EBIT adjusted for 2024 stood at €5.4 billion, reflecting commercial aircraft deliveries and strong helicopter performance, offset by charges in the space division and less favorable hedge rates.
  • Free cash flow before customer financing was €4.5 billion, supported by robust commercial momentum, although negatively impacted by the A400M program and inventory buildup for ramp-ups.
  • The net income for 2024 was €4.2 billion, with earnings per share reported at €5.36. Adjusted EPS stood at €5.05 based on an average of 790 million shares.

Q&A

  • Benjamin Heelan, Bank of America: Asked about Spirit AeroSystems' operational challenges. CFO Thomas Toepfer explained that a strike at Boeing and the need for additional investments to support ramp-ups for the A350 and A220 were key issues. He stated the integration of Spirit’s work packages is expected to close by mid-year.
  • David Perry, JPMorgan: Inquired about delivery guidance for 2025. CEO Guillaume Faury declined to specify model-level projections but highlighted bottlenecks with Spirit AeroSystems and engine suppliers affecting the A350 and A220 ramp-ups.
  • Doug Harned, Bernstein: Queried the A350 ramp-up impact from Spirit versus interior suppliers. Faury clarified that Spirit’s issues directly hinder ramp-up, while interior delays primarily affect delivery timing.
  • Ian Douglas-Pennant, UBS: Asked about the long-term cash flow outlook for Spirit AeroSystems. Toepfer stated that after investments and process improvements, Spirit’s work packages are expected to achieve cost parity with current external sourcing beyond 2028.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed skepticism regarding supply chain challenges, particularly Spirit AeroSystems, and sought clarity on operational and financial impacts. Questions reflected concerns about ramp-up feasibility for key programs like the A350 and A220.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone. CEO Guillaume Faury expressed confidence in meeting 2025 delivery targets but acknowledged ongoing bottlenecks. CFO Thomas Toepfer provided detailed explanations on financial impacts, including Spirit-related costs and dividend decisions.
  • Compared to prepared remarks, management was more defensive in addressing operational risks during the Q&A, particularly regarding supply chain constraints and Spirit AeroSystems’ integration.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • A comparison to the previous quarter is unavailable due to the lack of the prior quarter’s transcript.
  • Management emphasized consistent strategic priorities, including ramping up aircraft production, addressing supply chain challenges, and transforming the Defence and Space division.
  • Analysts’ tone remained focused on supply chain risks, reflecting ongoing concerns about operational execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted supply chain disruptions, particularly with Spirit AeroSystems and engine suppliers, as risks to achieving ramp-up targets for the A350 and A220.
  • Airbus faces potential challenges from tariffs, which could impact supply chain stability and financial performance.
  • Delays in the entry-into-service for the A350 freighter and ongoing issues with engine availability were noted as additional risks.

Final Takeaway

Airbus SE reported solid 2024 financial results, supported by strong commercial aircraft and helicopter deliveries. Looking ahead, the company targets 820 aircraft deliveries and €7 billion EBIT adjusted for 2025, despite ongoing supply chain challenges. Management remains focused on ramping up production and integrating Spirit AeroSystems' work packages to secure operational stability. Analysts raised concerns about supply chain risks, but management expressed confidence in navigating these challenges to achieve long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Live Nation Entertainment reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Live Nation Entertainment press release (NYSE:LYV): Q4 Revenue of $5.68B (-2.7% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Operating income of $825 million
  • Adjusted operating income of $2.15 billion
  • Adjusted operating income for concerts at a record high, up 65% to $530 million with margins of 2.8%
  • Concert attendance up 4%, with 151 million fans attending over 50k Live Nation events, up 9%
  • Sponsorship adjusted operating income of $764 million, up 13%

data.title: Texas Roadhouse tops estimates as comparable sales and margins shine
data.description:
Texas Roadhouse (NASDAQ:TXRH) slid past profit estimates with its Q4 earnings report on revenue growth of 23.3%.

Comparable restaurant sales increased 7.7% at company restaurants and increased 6.3% at domestic franchise restaurants. Average weekly sales at company restaurants were $153,867 of which $20,067 were to-go sales vs. average weekly sales of $141,653 of which $17,793 were to-go sales in the prior year.

Restaurant margin as a percentage of sales increased to 17.0% from 15.3% in the prior year, driven primarily by higher sales. The benefit of a higher average guest check, the benefit of the additional week, and improved labor productivity more than offset wage and other labor inflation of 5.0% and commodity inflation of 0.3%.

Nine company restaurants and five franchise restaurants were opened during the quarter.

"We ended 2024 on an incredible note, which was highlighted by fourth quarter and full-year traffic growth at all three of our brands." highlighted CEO Jerry Morgan.

Morgan said the company is off to a strong start in 2025 with the completion of the previously announced acquisition of 13 franchise restaurants. "In addition, due to the continued growth across our portfolio, our 800th restaurant is under construction and will open later this year," he added. Texas Roadhouse (NASDAQ:TXRH) plans to leverage the strength of its balance sheet to continue to fund development growth while also returning capital to our shareholders.

The new year is off to a solid start for Texas Roadhouse (TXRH). Comparable restaurant sales at company restaurants for the first seven weeks of FQ1 increased 2.9% compared to 2024. In addition, the company plans to implement a menu price increase of approximately 1.4% in early April.

Shares of Texas Roadhouse (TXRH) were down 1.0% in postmarket action.

data.title: MercadoLibre GAAP EPS of $12.61 beats by $5.05, revenue of $6.06B beats by $120M
data.description:
• MercadoLibre press release (NASDAQ:MELI): Q4 GAAP EPS of $12.61 beats by $5.05.
  • Revenue of $6.06B (+37.4% Y/Y) beats by $120M.
  • $58.9 billion Total Payment Volume, up 33% YoY and 49% FX-neutral $14.5 billion
  • Gross Merchandise Volume, up 8% YoY and 56% FX-neutral.
  • We ended 2024 with fantastic momentum and we are optimistic as we look ahead to 2025 and beyond. Investments that we have been making for years in our value proposition are bearing fruit and extending our competitive advantages. We are seeing tangible evidence of the power of the ecosystem as each part of it strengthens engagement in the others. This puts us in a unique position to explore the significant growth opportunities offered by the shift of offline retail online, by providing better financial services to millions of people and by the growing importance of first party data in advertising. We are as confident as ever that the best is yet to come.
  • Shares +13.24%.
